title: Change your phone number – Update your personal information with the CRA - Canada.ca
content:
Update your personal information with the CRA

## Update your personal information with the CRA - Change your phone number
Having an up-to-date telephone number with the CRA is important since it may be needed for multi-factor authentication (MFA) or when the CRA needs to contact you by phone.
For information about when the CRA may contact you and how to recognize a scam, visit Scams and fraud .

## Ways to update your phone number
Choose an option to learn how to make changes to your account.
Processing time: Immediate
Change your phone number online using your CRA account.
- Sign in to your CRA account
- Select your Individual account to access My Account
- Change the phone number the CRA will use: To contact you : Option 1 Go to Contact information To send your one-time passcode for MFA : Option 2 Scroll to Multi-factor authentication settings
- To contact you : Option 1 Go to Contact information

### To contact you : Option 1
Go to Contact information
- To send your one-time passcode for MFA : Option 2 Scroll to Multi-factor authentication settings

### To send your one-time passcode for MFA : Option 2
Scroll to Multi-factor authentication settings

## If you can't sign in to your CRA account
If your phone number has changed and you can't receive your multi-factor authentication (MFA) passcode to sign in, you will have to update your information by phone .
For more help with My Account, go to: Help with using your CRA account

## Make a change for someone else
To change a phone number online for someone else, including a spouse or family member, you must be an authorized representative and use Represent a Client in your CRA account .
Processing time: Immediate
- Social insurance number (SIN)
- Full name and date of birth
- Assessed tax return, notice of assessment or reassessment, or other tax document
You can also be signed in to your CRA account to access your tax information.

## Make a change for someone else
You can only call on behalf of someone else in the following 2 situations:
- The person is present on the call to verify their identity in English or French and provides verbal authorization for you to speak for them
The person is present on the call to verify their identity in English or French and provides verbal authorization for you to speak for them
- You are the person's legal representative with full legal authority to act as them For details: Authorize a representative
You are the person's legal representative with full legal authority to act as them
For details: Authorize a representative
The CRA only accepts collect calls made through a telephone operator. After your call is accepted by an automated response, you may hear a beep and notice a normal connection delay. This service operates in Eastern Standard Time.
Teletypewriter (TTY):
1-800-665-0354 (opens up tty application) 1-800-665-0354
Canada VRS (Video Relay Service):
Register with Canada VRS to download the app and call the VRS line
If you use any other operator-assisted relay service, call our regular telephone numbers on this page instead of the TTY or Canada VRS numbers.
Processing time: 4 to 6 weeks after the CRA receives your documents
Follow these steps to change your phone number by mail or fax:
- Complete Form RC325, Address and Telephone Number Change Request
- Mail or fax the completed form to your tax centre

## Make a change for someone else
To change the phone number of another individual, such as your spouse or common-law partner, you must be their legal representative. The form must be completed along with the name of the legal representative clearly mentioned with a signature.
- If you file electronically , at the same date and time your return is processed
- If you file a paper return , approximately 12 weeks after receiving your return
A home telephone number can be updated on your tax return when you file using any method.
For any other type of phone number, you must make the change another way (online, by phone or by mail).
However, you should not wait for tax filing season to update your information. In some cases, identification changes could have a direct impact on your eligibility for benefits.

## Change your phone number with other government departments
You need to contact each government organization that provides services to you to give them your new information.
The CRA will not share your new phone number or other personal information, including direct deposit, with other government departments.
Contact another Government of Canada department or agency

## Update other information
Change other personal details with the CRA, including your home or mailing address or email address.
Update your personal information with the CRA

### Give feedback about this page
Thank you for your feedback.
